seasonArc: Giulio Ciccone built his 2026 around the home Giro d'Italia, sharpening his climbing legs through the spring stage races and taking the mountains classification at the Volta a Catalunya. Arriving at the Giro as Lidl-Trek's lead climber, he set aside GC ambitions to chase stages and the maglia azzurra on Italian roads, animating the race relentlessly from the breakaways across the key mountain days. It paid off on the Dolomite queen stage (Stage 19), where he won the Cima Coppi atop the Passo Giau to seize the King of the Mountains classification from Vingegaard — and he carried the maglia azzurra to Rome to take the mountains title outright. A pure stage-hunting and climbing arc, delivered in front of his home crowds. He takes that form toward the summer's one-day classics and the World Championships road race, where he has previously finished in the top six.
